rule_text: AP works are executed by ONE OF FOUR methods: (i) Departmental method (engaging daily labour, supplying materials directly), (ii) Piece-work contract (rates without quantity/time reference, dept supervises), (iii) Lump-sum contract (complete work for fixed sum), (iv) Schedule contract (rates for items, payment by quantity executed); Lump-sum contract is the DEFAULT method except when another method is more advantageous.
verification_method: Check tender BDS specifies one of the 4 methods; verify justification if method != Lump-sum

rule_text: No AP Government servant may enter into a contract for execution of a work UNLESS funds have been duly provided OR an assurance is received from the competent funding authority that funds will be allotted before the liability matures.
verification_method: Check tender file: budget allocation OR fund-allotment-assurance letter on file before contract signing date

rule_text: No AP work to be executed under contract should be STARTED until the contractor has signed a FORMAL WRITTEN AGREEMENT (except emergency starts under Article 170 or petty works under Article 167); for petty works <= Rs.1,000 a written understanding specifying prices and rates may suffice.
verification_method: Check formal written agreement signed by contractor exists prior to work-start date; for petty works <= Rs.1,000 written-understanding may suffice
